title: plagiobothrys medius
intro: Plagiobothrys medius, also known as small-flowered popcorn flower, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family. It is a small, annual herb with small, white or pale blue flowers that have a distinctive popcorn-like appearance, often growing in disturbed areas. The plant is used in traditional medicine, with medicinal properties attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antihistamine effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of allergies, colds, and respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Plagiobothrys medius include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water.
